title: What is OCR? Reading Pixels as Text
concept: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) converts images of typed, handwritten, or printed text into machine-encoded text, operating via detection and recognition.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main benefit of bottom-up pose estimation?

options.text: It keeps execution speed constant even as more people enter the image.
It is less complex than top-down.

exercises.explain: Correct! Because it detects all joints in a single forward pass, bottom-up models are highly efficient for crowded scenes.

cards.title: Unlocking paper files
cards.body: If you take a photo of a page in a book, your computer doesn't know what words are on it. To the computer, it's just a matrix of colored pixels. If you want to search, copy, or translate the text, you need a system that can look at those visual shapes and translate them back into digital letters. This is the magic of OCR.

cards.title: What is OCR?
cards.body: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) is the technology that converts images of text (such as scanned documents, photos of signs, or handwritten notes) into editable, searchable machine text. A standard OCR pipeline has two stages: Text Localization (finding where the words are) and Text Recognition (reading the letters).

cards.title: Transcribing audio
cards.body: Think of OCR like writing down what someone is saying. First, you listen to find where the speech is in the audio (localization). Then, you listen closely to decode the exact words and write them down on a page (recognition). If you can't hear where they are speaking, you can't transcribe them.

cards.title: Live camera translation
cards.body: When you point your smartphone camera at a restaurant menu in a foreign country, Google Translate localizes the foreign text, reads the characters, translates them, and overlays the translated English words directly onto the image.

cards.title: It's not just template matching
cards.body: Early OCR systems used simple templates: they looked for a perfect 'A' shape. If the font changed slightly or the page was rotated, they failed. Modern OCR uses deep neural networks that understand the abstract strokes and curves of letters, making them robust to different handwriting styles and fonts.

cards.title: Visual Shapes vs. String Code
cards.body: How data changes during OCR processing:

left.text:
RGB pixel matrix of shapes
Cannot be searched or edited
High memory footprint

right.text:
Unicode characters (e.g. 'Hello')
Searchable and copy-pasteable
Tiny byte footprint

exercises.prompt: What are the two primary stages of a modern OCR pipeline?

options.text: Text Localization (finding text) and Text Recognition (reading text)
Image resizing and color space conversion
Gram matrix computation and style transfer

exercises.explain: OCR pipelines first locate text regions in the frame, and then transcribe the pixels inside those regions into digital strings.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Simple template-matching OCR is highly robust to variations in fonts, rotation, and handwriting.
exercises.explain: False. Template matching requires perfect visual alignment; deep learning models are needed to handle font variations and handwriting.

exercises.sentence: The first step of an OCR pipeline, which locates the text regions, is called text ___.
exercises.explain: Text localization determines the coordinates and bounding boxes of words or characters in an image.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a text recognition task?

options.text: Transcribing a crop of handwritten text into a digital text file
Finding where paragraphs are on a scanned book page
Running a blur filter over license plates

exercises.explain: Transcribing crop pixels into digital characters (strings) is the definition of text recognition.

exercises.prompt: You are building an automated system to process paper invoices. The system needs to read the total cost from scanned images and input it into accounting software. How should you design this pipeline?

options.text: Locate the text regions using a detector, crop the invoice sections, transcribe the crops using a text recognizer, and extract the dollar amount.
Classify the entire invoice image directly as '$150' or '$200' using flat classification.
Run face recognition on the logo of the company.
Apply style transfer to make the invoice look like a painting.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The standard OCR pipeline requires localizing the text regions first, then transcribing the cropped pixels into strings, and parsing the numeric values. Great! Next: how we localize text using specialized detectors.
